reasoning: Johannes Kaiser is polling 4th as of late October 2025, just weeks before the November 16 election. Polling averages (such as El Pais, Oct 21) place him at roughly 12-13.4%, trailing right-wing frontrunner José Antonio Kast (~23%), center-right candidate Evelyn Matthei (~16%), and left-wing leader Jeannette Jara (~29.5%). To win the first round, Kaiser would have to receive the most valid votes. The probability of him overcoming a 15+ point deficit within three weeks, requiring the collapse of two major candidates on his own side of the spectrum and the leading opponent, is practically zero. Therefore, the chance is modeled at the floor probability of 2%.
